data.title: Swiss Building Materials Group Holcim Eyes Profitable Growth Under New Strategy
data.description:
Holcim on Friday unveiled its new strategy, setting its sights on profitable growth in the "most attractive" markets and bolstering its position in sustainable construction following the planned spin-off of its North American business.

Under its NextGen Growth 2030 strategy, the Swiss building materials group plans to invest 18 billion francs to 22 billion francs between 2025 and 2030, focusing on organic growth and value-accretive mergers and acquisitions.

The group aims to double-down on the "most attractive" markets by expanding further in Latin America to capitalize on industrialization trends and large-scale construction projects, while delivering profitable growth in Europe on the back of increasing demand for its sustainable, Australia and North Africa.

The company is targeting over 20 million tons of recycled construction demolition materials in 2030 and intends to scale up its sustainable product lines to drive higher profits. Additionally, it aims to slash scope 1 net emissions by 30%, compared with 2020 levels, and reduce freshwater withdrawal as part of its sustainability goals.

Holcim is also betting on the expansion of its high-value Building Solutions segment, targeting 50% of net sales by growing its presence in foundation, flooring, walling and roofing systems. The strategic move is expected to benefit from the booming demand for repair and renovations.

"Holcim is best positioned through NextGen Growth 2030 to benefit from the powerful megatrends shaping the future of construction, from urbanization to energy-efficient refurbishment," said Chief Executive Miljan Gutovic. "Expanding high-value Building Solutions is a key part of Holcim's transformation. With this new strategy, we are unlocking significant growth opportunities to drive shareholder value."

As part of its 2030 financial targets, the group aims to achieve an average of 3% to 5% annual net sales growth and 6% to 10% recurring EBIT growth.

As of late Friday morning, Holcim's shares were trading almost 1% higher.

data.title: Japan's Housing Starts Fall in January
data.description:
Japan's housing starts fell steeper by 4.6% year over year in January after a 2.5% fall in December 2024, the Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism reported Friday.

Construction starts of owned houses slipped 8.6% reversing the 4.6% gain a month earlier. Construction starts of rented homes slipped 1.2% after a 2.1% increase last December.

Meanwhile, construction starts of issued homes slipped 0.3% in January, compared with the nearly 45% growth the month prior.

Built-for-sale construction starts fell 6%, while prefabricated starts gained 1.2%. Two-by-four housing starts decreased by 6%.

data.title: Portugal Retail Sales Maintain Solid Growth
data.description:
Retail sales in Portugal rose by 5% year-on-year in February 2025, following an upwardly revised 5.5% increase in the previous month, indicating a strong pace of growth.

Increases were seen for both food, beverages and tobacco (6.1% vs 7.6% in January) and non-food products (4.3% vs 4%).

On a monthly basis, retail sales were flat, after an upwardly revised 0.2% rise in the prior month.

data.title: US Automobile Safety Regulator to Investigate Honda Engine Restart Complaints
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

The U.S. automobile safety regulator has opened up an investigation into more than 2 million Honda vehicles over reports that the engine can fail to restart from an idle position.

The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, or NHTSA, will investigate complaints that allege that the engine fails to turn on its own from a complete stop at a traffic light or road intersection with the auto start-stop feature engaged.

In the document dated March 26, the regulator said that some of the complainants allege that a jump start was required for the vehicle to continue on the journey.

The NHTSA said this affects about 2.21 million vehicles which include popular models such as the Honda Odyssey as well as certain Acura models.

While the document noted that Honda has released service bulletins in January of 2023, the Office of Defects investigation continued to receive complaints that the auto start-stop function continued to fail, allegedly even after work had been done to remedy the problem.

The NHTSA said the engineering analysis is being opened to further consider the potential safety defect, including gathering additional data regarding Honda's service campaign efficacy.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Michelin Target Raised to EUR38 From EUR36 by HSBC
Michelin Raised to Buy From Hold by HSBC
Apple and Meta Platforms Could Get Some Regulatory Relief. It's All Thanks to Trump
data.description:
By Adam Clark

Apple and Meta Platforms are facing the likelihood of regulatory fines. However, the blow could be less than feared due the policies of President Donald Trump.

Apple and Meta Platforms are both expected to face monetary penalties from the European Union for breaches of its Digital Markets Act next week but the fines will be minimal as the EU looks to avoid a spat with the Trump administration, the Financial Times reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The Digital Markets Act designates companies including Apple and Meta as running "gatekeeper" platforms. The companies have to comply with certain conditions such as sharing data with rivals and making their services interoperable with competitors.

If officials determine that a company has broken the EU's Digital Markets Act, it could be fined up to 10% of its worldwide revenue. However, Apple and Meta are likely to face penalties far below that level, according to the report.

The European Commission didn't immediately respond to a request for comment on Friday.

Trump said earlier this year that he would consider tariffs in response to "digital services taxes, fines, practices, and policies" imposed on American companies. Some of the biggest European economies, including France, have passed taxes on revenue that multinational tech firms reap within their territory.

Apple will likely be fined and ordered to revise its App Store rules over an investigation into whether it prevented app developers from directing consumers to offers elsewhere, while Meta will be hit with a fine and ordered to change its model which requires European users to pay a monthly fee for ad-free versions of its platforms if they wish to opt out of targeted advertising based on their data, the report said.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Glass Lewis ESG Controversy Alert: Krung Thai Bank
data.description:
Glass Lewis issued a controversy alert on Thursday for Krung Thai Bank shareholders ahead of the company's annual shareholder meeting on April 4.

The alert relates to the Thailand-based bank's failure to divulge the proposed nominees for election in a timely manner, Glass Lewis said.

The bank has also given members of the board and the company's auditor excessive payments in terms of bonuses and non-audit related services, according to Glass Lewis.

The alert's rationale relates to egregious governance practices and falls under board effectiveness, oversight issue, and other governance issue.

data.title: Aimia Q4 Net Loss Narrows, Provides Guidance, Names New Executive Chairman
data.description:
Aimia on Friday reported a narrower fourth-quarter net loss as revenue increased.

Th net loss came in at $41.2 million, or $0.48 per share, compared with a loss of $59 million, or $0.69 per share. Revenue rose to $127.2 million from $100.1 million.

Adjusted EBITDA swung to $17.3 million from a loss of $1.1 million.

Aimia expects 2025 adjusted EBITDA at its two global businesses, Bozzetto and Cortland, on a combined basis at $88 million to $95 million, compared with $80.4 million in 2024. Bozzetto is a sustainable specialty chemicals company and Cortland International is a rope and netting solutions company.

The company also named Rhys Summerton as executive chairman following the retirement of Tom Finke.

data.title: Stallion India Fluorochemicals CFO Resigns
data.description:
Stallion India Fluorochemicals (NSE:Stallion, ) said Ashish Mehta resigned as chief financial officer and key managerial officer of the company due to personal reasons, according to a filing with the Indian bourses on Friday.

The resignation will be effective April 7, the filing said.

data.title: MODV INVESTOR ALERT: Bronstein, Gewirtz and Grossman, LLC Reminds Investors of ModivCare, Inc. to Contact the Firm Today!
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / March 28, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC, a nationally recognized law firm, notifies investors that a class action lawsuit has been filed against ModivCare, Inc. ("ModivCare" or "the Company") and certain of its officers.

Class Definition

This lawsuit seeks to recover damages against Defendants for alleged violations of the federal securities laws on behalf of all persons and entities that purchased or otherwise acquired ModivCare securities between November 3, 2022 and September 15, 2024, both dates inclusive (the "Class Period"). Such investors are encouraged to join this case by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/MODV.

Case Details

The Complaint alleges that, throughout the Class Period, the Defendants made materially false and/or misleading statements, as well as failed to disclose adverse facts about the Company's business, operations, and prospects. Specifically, the Complaint alleges that Defendants failed to disclose that certain contracts used in ModivCare's NEMT segment caused the Company's free cash flow to deteriorate and that, as a result, (1) contract renegotiations and pricing accommodations negatively impacted the Company's adjusted EBITDA; (2) the Company had insufficient liquidity; and (3) Defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis.

What's Next?

A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to review a copy of the Complaint, you can visit the firm's site: bgandg.com/MODV. or you may contact Peretz Bronstein, Esq. If you suffered a loss in ModivCare you have until March 31, 2025, to request that the Court appoint you as lead plaintiff. Your ability to share in any recovery doesn't require that you serve as lead plaintiff.

There is No Cost to You

We represent investors in class actions on a contingency fee basis. That means we will ask the court to reimburse us for out-of-pocket expenses and attorneys' fees, usually a percentage of the total recovery, only if we are successful.

Why Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman

Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is a nationally recognized firm that represents investors in securities fraud class actions and shareholder derivative suits. Our firm has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors nationwide.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn, X, Facebook, or Instagram.

Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

Contact

Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC

Peretz Bronstein or Nathan Miller

SOURCE: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: IT and financials drag Sri Lankan shares lower
data.description:
Sri Lankan shares closed lower on Friday, dragged by information technology and financial stocks.

* The CSE All-Share index settled 0.42% lower at 15,814.82 points.

* Sri Lanka's Colombo consumer price index fell 2.6% year-on-year in March, the statistics department said on Friday.

* The Nuwara Eliya Hotels Company and Ceylinco Holdings were the top losers by index points, down 144 points and 55 points, respectively, on the day.

* Trading volume on the index rose to 52.9 million shares from 47.37 million shares in the previous session.

* The equity market's turnover dipped to 1.65 billion Sri Lankan rupees from 2.14 billion rupees in the previous session, according to exchange data.

* Foreign investors were net sellers, offloading stocks worth 218.73 million rupees, while domestic investors were net buyers, purchasing shares worth 1.48 billion rupees, the data showed.

* Sri Lanka financial markets will remain closed on Monday for a holiday.

* For a report on global markets, click

data.title: Craft Sportswear Signs Partnership Agreement with IFA New England
data.description:
Intercontinental Football Academy (IFA) New England, Challenger Teamwear, and Craft Sportswear North America have signed a multi-year partnership agreement to provide IFA New England's players and coaches with match kits and training apparel. This collaboration unites three organizations with a shared commitment and dedication to delivering top-quality products, excellent service, and a first-class experience for athletes.

Intercontinental Football Academy (IFA) New England is one of the largest soccer clubs in the USA, with approximately 4,000 active youth players. The agreement spans five years.

The agreement marks the first step in New Wave Group's teamwear initiative in North America.

- In recent years, we have developed a teamwear offering for various sports in the European market. It is exciting to see that our strategy also fits the American market, and after just three months on the continent, one of the largest soccer clubs in the USA has chosen Craft as a partner for the next five years, says Stefan Persson, CEO of Craft of Scandinavia AB.

About Craft Sportswear

Craft Sportswear operates in 70 markets worldwide. In soccer and team sports, the brand has agreements with thousands of clubs and collaborates with clubs such as IFK Göteborg, Hammarby IF, Trelleborgs FF, Varbergs Bois, Växjö DFF, KAA Gent (Belgium), Fredrikstad (Norway), AGF Århus (Denmark), Almere City (Netherlands), SV Darmstadt 98 (Germany), AC Oulo, and Ekenäs IF (Finland). Additionally, Craft collaborates with the Swedish national handball team and 15 clubs in the top tier handball leagues, more than ten elite basketball teams, the Swedish and Danish national volleyball teams, among others. Craft also partners with the Swedish, Norwegian, and French national cross-country skiing teams, the Swedish national table tennis team, the Danish and Finnish national athletics teams, and the Norwegian and French national biathlon teams. Craft Sportswear is part of the New Wave Group.

About Challenger Teamwear

Challenger Teamwear, headquartered in Lenexa, Kansas, offers a comprehensive range of sportswear and equipment, providing personalized and efficient solutions for sports organizations across the USA. With a commitment to supporting their partners and thinking outside the box, Challenger Teamwear constantly finds new ways to help teams and clubs succeed both on and off the pitch.

About Intercontinental Football Academy New England

Intercontinental Football Academy (IFA) New England is based in Massachusetts, USA. With a large, dedicated player base across the region, IFA New England offers top-class soccer training for both boys and girls, combining a player-first approach with a commitment to development. With a strong European influence and a diverse player base, IFA promotes a soccer culture rooted in quality, ambition, and innovation. Their teams compete at the highest levels and work to develop elite players while strengthening the broader soccer community.

data.title: BMO Capital Upgrades Hudson Pacific Properties to Outperform From Market Perform, Adjusts PT to $5 From $4
data.description: Hudson Pacific Properties has an average rating of hold and mean price target of $3.92, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Market Chatter: United States Steel, Nippon Steel Offer Increased Investment to White House to Close Proposed Merger
data.description:
United States Steel and Nippon Steel are discussing with White House officials ways to preserve their proposed $14 billion merger and secure billions more in investments from the Japanese steelmaker into Rust Belt sites, Semafor reported Friday, citing unnamed sources familiar with the talks.

Nippon Steel has reportedly offered to increase the $2.7 billion it previously proposed to upgrade US Steel facilities, with some saying it could be upped to as much as $7 billion.

Under the new terms, Nippon would retain its offer to acquire the US steel manufacturer for $55 per share, the people told Semafor.

US Steel, Nippon, and the White House did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' requests for comment.

US Steel's stock rose 6.3% in recent Friday premarket activity.
